title: Structured Health Reset Flow
description: Guided pathway to reset household health patterns: baseline metrics, nutrition alignment, kitchen and storehouse reset, movement-friendly routines, and follow-through.
objectives: Capture a realistic health baseline for the household (weight, energy, key symptoms).
Align meals, ingredients, and storehouse inventory with updated health goals.
Reset key environments (kitchen and sleep spaces) to support the reset.
Increase the proportion of health-aligned meals cooked at home.
Establish small repeatable routines that maintain the new baseline.

entryCriteria.description: When to consider activating this health reset flow.

exitCriteria.description: When the health reset is considered complete (for now).

nowCtaBehavior.description: How the 'Now' button should behave when this flow is active.

assessment.label: Baseline Health Assessment
assessment.goal: Capture a snapshot of current health, eating patterns, and storehouse realities.

steps.label: Record Health Snapshot
steps.description: Log current weight, energy levels, major symptoms, and doctor notes to anchor the reset.
steps.nowCtaHint: If no recent health snapshot exists, this should be the first suggestion.

steps.label: Check BMI and Target Range
steps.description: Use a BMI or body composition calculator to understand current status and set realistic targets.
steps.nowCtaHint: Optional but recommended if weight has shifted or goals have changed.

steps.label: Update Macro & Micronutrient Targets
steps.description: Review daily macro and micronutrient needs for the household (including hair/skin goals if used).
steps.nowCtaHint: These targets will drive which meals and recipes are encouraged in later phases.

nutrition_alignment.label: Nutrition & Storehouse Alignment
nutrition_alignment.goal: Align the storehouse, recipes, and meal planning with updated health targets.

steps.label: Audit Storehouse for Health Alignment
steps.description: Score current inventory on how well it supports the new health targets (whole foods vs. ultra-processed).
steps.nowCtaHint: High junk density should push this step to the top of the queue.

steps.label: Deprioritize or Re-route Junk Foods
steps.description: Flag or reclassify items that work against health goals, and decide what to phase out.
steps.nowCtaHint: Can suggest using some items in low-frequency recipes instead of daily snacks.

steps.label: Create Health-Focused Meal Plan
steps.description: Generate a 7–14 day meal plan using health presets (e.g., blood sugar friendly, heart support, hair nutrition).
steps.nowCtaHint: Primary planning step; recommended before any batch cooking sessions.

steps.label: Generate Targeted Health Shopping List
steps.description: Create a list focused on adding missing whole foods and healthy fats, not random extras.
steps.nowCtaHint: Use if budget allows a focused health restock trip.

steps.label: Align Garden & Animals with Health Goals
steps.description: Adjust garden crops and animal stocking to support long-term access to lean proteins, greens, and key micronutrients.
steps.nowCtaHint: Optional but powerful if you’re growing or raising part of your own food.

kitchen_environment_reset.label: Kitchen & Sleep Environment Reset
kitchen_environment_reset.goal: Reset key environments so it’s easier to eat well, rest, and recover.

steps.label: Run Kitchen Health Reset Session
steps.description: Deep clean counters, stove, fridge, and key tools so healthy cooking feels easier and safer.
steps.nowCtaHint: Strong candidate for 'Now' immediately after health-focused meal planning.

steps.label: Reorganize Pantry for Healthy Choices
steps.description: Place whole foods and healthy ingredients at eye level; move treats to lower priority zones.
steps.nowCtaHint: Lightweight step that can ride along with a cleaning session.

steps.label: Reset Sleep Space
steps.description: Clean and declutter sleep areas (linen, dust, lighting) to support better rest and recovery.
steps.nowCtaHint: Suggested when low energy is flagged as a major complaint.

routine_build.label: Health-Supporting Routine Build
routine_build.goal: Turn the plan into sustainable daily/weekly routines: cooking, movement, and light cleaning.

steps.label: Run Health-Focused Batch Cooking Session
steps.description: Cook key meals and components (proteins, grains, veggies) so healthy choices are easy on busy days.
steps.nowCtaHint: Top candidate for 'Now' when a health-focused meal plan is present.

steps.label: Schedule Light Movement Sessions via Household Tasks
steps.description: Use garden work, cleaning, and kitchen prep sessions as structured, low-impact movement blocks.
steps.nowCtaHint: Can schedule multiple small movement-friendly sessions across the week.

steps.label: Daily Kitchen Reset Lite
steps.description: Add a short, repeatable end-of-day cleaning session to prevent buildup and keep cooking approachable.
steps.nowCtaHint: Often suggested after evening cooking sessions to protect tomorrow’s momentum.

steps.label: Set Habit Anchors & Reminders
steps.description: Link key health actions (drinking water, prep veggies, stretch) to existing household routines and schedule reminders.
steps.nowCtaHint: Small configuration step; increases the value of every future session.

reflection_and_adjustment.label: Reflection & Adjustment
reflection_and_adjustment.goal: Review progress after a few weeks and adjust recipes, routines, and storehouse patterns.

steps.label: Repeat Health Snapshot
steps.description: Re-log weight, energy, symptoms, and compare to baseline to see if the reset is working.
steps.nowCtaHint: Suggest this after 2–4 weeks of running health reset routines.

steps.label: Adjust Meal Plan from Feedback
steps.description: Tune recipes and meal frequency based on what the household actually enjoyed and tolerated well.
steps.nowCtaHint: Fine-tune instead of starting over; keeps the reset sustainable.

steps.label: Lock In Health-Friendly Favorites
steps.description: Tag favorite recipes and batch sessions as 'health default' to be prioritized in future flows.
steps.nowCtaHint: Encourages repeating the most successful meals rather than chasing novelty.
